_metadata.description: Photonics parameters for three-zone metasurface AR waveguide in-coupler
_metadata.primary_reference: Goodsell et al. 2024, Opt. Express 32(6), 9967-9981. DOI: 10.1364/OE.515544
_metadata.adapting_reference: Xiong et al. 2025, Opt. Mater. Express 15(12), 3129-3140. DOI: 10.1364/OME.576634
_metadata.notes: Goodsell 2024 full-text is behind Optica paywall (JS-gated, no open-access PDF found). Per-zone efficiency targets marked REQUIRES_PAPER_ACCESS are from the paper body/Fig 2(e) and could not be scraped. All refractive index values are analytically computed from verified Sellmeier coefficients or sourced from peer-reviewed datasets.

waveguide_geometry.source: Goodsell et al. 2024 abstract and Xiong et al. 2025 paper description
waveguide_geometry.confidence: HIGH - stated in paper abstracts and Optica news release

per_zone_efficiency_targets._note: Exact values are in Goodsell 2024 Fig 2(e) / body text. Paper is paywalled; Optica pages return JavaScript loading screen and no open-access PDF was found via Unpaywall, Semantic Scholar, or arXiv. Values below are PLACEHOLDERS derived from the theoretical framework described in the abstract.
per_zone_efficiency_targets._framework_summary: Goodsell 2024 derives a framework where per-zone efficiency targets are computed from irradiance maps, efficiency maps, and MTF constraints. The architecture meets the fundamental in-coupling efficiency limit while achieving diffraction-limited MTF to 30 cycles/deg (20/20 vision), computed 2000x faster than commercial ray tracers.

zone_1.description: Zone closest to waveguide edge (smallest field angle contribution)

zone_3.description: Zone closest to center (largest field angle contribution)

overall_average_efficiency.source: Xiong et al. 2025 via Optica news release

edge_field_efficiency.source: Xiong et al. 2025 via Optica news release

N_BK7_at_532nm.computation_method: Analytical - Sellmeier equation (SCHOTT N-BK7 data sheet)

TiO2_rutile_bulk_at_532nm.source: refractiveindex.info - Devore 1951, J. Opt. Soc. Am. 41, 416-419
TiO2_rutile_bulk_at_532nm.confidence: HIGH - analytically computed from peer-reviewed dataset

notes: Siefke et al. 2016 (refractiveindex.info) measured 350nm ALD TiO2 film; full tabulated data requires CSV download from refractiveindex.info (page blocked direct numerical extraction)
Vazquez-Arce et al. 2024 (Adv. Mater. Interfaces) reports n=2.15-2.18 at 632nm for H2O-based ALD TiO2; at 532nm (shorter wavelength) n is slightly higher due to normal dispersion
ACS Appl. Nano Mater. 2023 (Jang et al.) reports n=2.15 at 543nm after 15-cycle ALD treatment on TiO2 nanocrystal films
Bulk rutile n=2.668 at 532nm (computed above); ALD amorphous films are consistently lower by 0.25-0.35
Metasurface/metalens literature (e.g., Devlin, Capasso group) typically quotes n~2.35 for amorphous ALD TiO2 at visible wavelengths
TiO2_ALD_amorphous_at_532nm.source_Siefke: Siefke et al. 2016 - refractiveindex.info, ALD TiO2 350nm film
TiO2_ALD_amorphous_at_532nm.source_Wiley: Vazquez-Arce et al. 2024, Adv. Mater. Interfaces, DOI:10.1002/admi.202400269
TiO2_ALD_amorphous_at_532nm.confidence: MEDIUM - range from multiple sources; exact value at 532nm requires downloading Siefke CSV or ellipsometry data

TiO2_anatase_ALD_at_532nm.notes: Anatase TiO2 (crystallized by annealing) has higher n than amorphous; less common for room-temp ALD metasurfaces

Goodsell_2024_fulltext.status: PAYWALLED - Optica site is JavaScript-gated; all fulltext/viewmedia URLs return 202 'Please wait...' with 14 chars content
Goodsell_2024_fulltext.open_access: CC BY licensed (GOLD OA per Semantic Scholar) but PDF URL resolves to same JS-gated page
Goodsell_2024_fulltext.recommendation: Access via institutional subscription OR direct author contact (Jeremy Goodsell, University of Rochester)

Xiong_2025_fulltext.recommendation: Access via institutional subscription; contains Fig 2(e) adapted from Goodsell 2024

summary_of_confirmed_values.per_zone_targets: NOT RETRIEVED - requires Goodsell 2024 full paper access
